project: Bike Garage - Phase 2 Interactivity and Polish
overview: Extend the Phase 1 MVP with an interactive image experience: users can click on parts of the bike photo, which highlights and zooms into that area and shows the detailed analysis for that part. Adds a reanalysis button and refines responsive layout for mobile and desktop.
goals: Enable click interaction on the bike photo to identify and highlight specific parts
Display detailed part info panel when a part is clicked
Add a reanalysis button to trigger a fresh Claude Vision call on the same photo
Refine responsive design for both desktop and mobile web
nonGoals: No data persistence — all state is still in-memory/session only
No manual part addition or editing by the user
successMetrics: Clicking a part on the bike image highlights that area with a visible overlay
Part detail panel appears within 100ms of click (no async call required — data is already loaded)
Reanalysis button triggers a new Claude Vision call and replaces results in-place
Layout is usable and unbroken on a 375px mobile viewport
openQuestions: Should Claude Vision return bounding box coordinates for parts in Phase 2, or is click-based region detection approximate?
If bounding boxes are used, are they normalized (0-1) or pixel-based relative to original image dimensions?

stack.newDependencies: None expected — Phase 2 is primarily frontend interaction work

routes.name: Results (interactive)
routes.purpose: Bike photo with clickable part overlays, detail panel, reanalysis button

uiNotes: Results page left column: bike photo displayed with SVG or div overlays for each part bounding region
Hovering a part region shows a subtle highlight ring
Clicking a part region activates it: bold highlight + zoom-in animation on that region
Part detail panel (right side or modal on mobile) shows: part name, component group, brand/model, condition badge, priority badge, condition notes, pricing results
Active part is also highlighted in the parts table on the right
Reanalysis button: positioned above the photo, triggers POST /api/analyze with same image data
Mobile layout: photo on top full-width, part detail panel below photo, parts table below that
Zoom effect: CSS transform scale on a clipped container — should not reflow other layout elements

fields: boundingBox: { x: 0-1, y: 0-1, width: 0-1, height: 0-1 } (normalized, relative to image)

rules: Claude Vision prompt must be updated to include bounding box coordinates for each visible part (normalized 0.0–1.0 relative to image dimensions)
Parts with visible_in_image=false should not render a clickable overlay region
Only one part can be active/highlighted at a time — clicking another part deselects the previous one
Clicking outside all part regions deselects the active part
Reanalysis replaces all analysis and pricing data in state — there is no comparison or history view
Zoom effect must be reversible (clicking again or clicking outside deactivates zoom)

stories.title: Update Claude Vision prompt to return bounding box coordinates per part
stories.description: As the backend, I want Claude Vision to return normalized bounding box coordinates for each identified part so the frontend can render clickable overlays on the photo.
acceptanceCriteria: Claude prompt updated to request a boundingBox field for each part: { x, y, width, height } normalized 0.0–1.0 relative to image dimensions
Prompt instructs: x and y are the top-left corner of the bounding region, width and height are the region dimensions
Schema validation updated to accept optional boundingBox on each part (not all parts may have a detectable region)
Parts with no detectable region have boundingBox: null
POST /api/analyze response now includes boundingBox in each part object
Existing npm tests updated to reflect new schema shape
Example: Rear Derailleur part → boundingBox: { x: 0.6, y: 0.55, width: 0.12, height: 0.1 }
Negative case: part with visible_in_image=false → boundingBox is null

stories.title: Render clickable part overlay regions on bike photo
stories.description: As a user, I want to see subtle clickable regions on the bike photo corresponding to identified parts so I know where to click for details.
acceptanceCriteria: BikeImageOverlay component renders the bike photo with absolutely-positioned div or SVG overlay regions for each part that has a non-null boundingBox
Overlay regions are positioned using normalized boundingBox coordinates scaled to the rendered image dimensions
On hover, overlay region shows a semi-transparent highlight ring (outline, not fill) so the photo remains visible
Cursor changes to pointer over clickable regions
Regions with visible_in_image=false or null boundingBox are not rendered
Image and overlays scale correctly when the browser window is resized
npm run lint passes
Example: 8-part analysis with 6 parts having bounding boxes → 6 overlay regions visible on photo
Negative case: analysis with 0 bounding boxes → photo renders normally with no overlays, no errors

stories.title: Click a part region to highlight, zoom, and show detail panel
stories.description: As a user, I want to click a part region on the photo to zoom into that area and see detailed information for that part so I can quickly understand its condition and priority.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking a part overlay region sets that part as 'active' in component state
Active region is highlighted with a more prominent overlay (colored border matching condition color)
Photo container applies a CSS transform to zoom and pan to center the active part's bounding box region (scale ~2x, clipped to container)
Zoom/pan uses CSS transition for a smooth animation (200–300ms ease)
Part detail panel updates immediately to show: part name, component group, brand/model, condition badge, priority badge, condition notes, and its pricing results
Corresponding row in the parts table is highlighted (scroll into view if needed)
Clicking the active region again, or clicking outside all regions, deactivates zoom and returns photo to normal scale
Only one part can be active at a time
Example: click 'Rear Derailleur' overlay → photo zooms to that area, detail panel shows Shimano Claris with 'fair' condition badge
Negative case: rapidly clicking two different part regions → second click correctly replaces first, no visual glitch

stories.title: Part detail panel component
stories.description: As a user, I want a dedicated detail panel that shows full information for the selected part, including its pricing results, so I don't have to scan the full table.
acceptanceCriteria: PartDetailPanel component receives the active part object and its pricing results as props
Panel displays: part name (heading), component group, brand and model, condition badge, priority badge, condition notes (full text)
Pricing section shows up to 3 Performance Bicycle results: product title (linked), price, availability
If pricing results are empty, shows 'No results found on Performance Bicycle'
Panel shows a 'No part selected — click a part on the photo' placeholder when no part is active
On mobile (< 768px), panel renders as a bottom sheet or modal overlay instead of a side column
npm run lint passes
Example: active part is a brake caliper with 2 pricing results → panel shows both with prices and links
Negative case: active part has no pricing results → panel still renders correctly with the 'no results' message

stories.title: Reanalysis button — trigger fresh Claude Vision call
stories.description: As a user, I want a reanalysis button that sends the same uploaded photo back to Claude Vision so I can get a fresh analysis without re-uploading.
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Reanalyze' button is displayed on the Results page above the bike photo
Clicking the button POSTs the same image file (held in component state or re-read from a stored File object) to POST /api/analyze
During reanalysis, button shows 'Reanalyzing…' and is disabled; a loading overlay covers the photo
On success, all analysis state is replaced: parts list, bike metadata, active selection, and pricing results are all cleared and reloaded
Pricing is automatically re-fetched after reanalysis completes (same as initial flow)
On error, show inline error 'Reanalysis failed. Please try again.' without losing the previous results
If the user navigated to results from a fresh upload, the original File object is available in React Router state for reuse
npm test includes a test that the reanalysis endpoint call is made with the correct content type
Example: click Reanalyze → loading overlay → new parts appear (may differ from first run)
Negative case: reanalysis fails due to network error → previous results remain visible with error message shown

stories.title: Responsive layout refinement — desktop and mobile web
stories.description: As a user, I want the app to be fully usable on both desktop (1280px+) and mobile web (375px) so I can use it on the go.
acceptanceCriteria: Desktop (≥1024px): two-column layout — left column: photo with overlays + reanalyze button; right column: part detail panel + scrollable parts table
Tablet (768–1023px): same two-column layout, narrower columns
Mobile (<768px): single column — photo full width on top, part detail panel (bottom sheet on click), parts table below
No horizontal scroll at any breakpoint from 375px to 1920px
Touch targets for part overlay regions are at least 44x44px on mobile (overlay region minimum size enforced)
Bottom sheet on mobile slides up with animation when a part is selected, dismisses on tap outside
Parts table on mobile uses a condensed single-line row format (name + priority badge only, expand on tap for details)
npm run lint passes
Example: open on iPhone SE (375px) → upload works, results display in single column, tapping a region opens bottom sheet
Negative case: rotate device to landscape (667px) → layout switches to two-column mid-width, no overflow
